ending: John returns to Sutton Farm after reclaiming the ornate box and tower token from Catherine. Reese and Sandra die fighting Thomas Little, Ferdie, and Sigvald, while Ellie kills Grimes with a curse and survives the resulting mana injury after John intervenes. Haver reaches the farm alive, sees that its defenders have prevailed, and withdraws. The household and dairy remain secure, with Thomas and Evan working alongside John, Ellie, and Ben. Red Coral's assault is ended, but Catherine's fate is unconfirmed and Earl Vosik remains connected to the tower scheme. Ellie learns that her interrupted class ceremony may still permit a future magical class, probably involving water, while Ben is revealed not to be her biological brother. John identifies Tower Master Kelvis as Storm Master Kelvis and opens the recovered box to find six water-magic spell scrolls. Kelvis's testament points toward an undiscovered inverted tower containing his student's research and connects that work to the world-changing summoning. John still must develop food that can withstand mana and prevent his doom total from reaching the apocalypse threshold.
in_short: John Sutton, a discharged mage seeking retirement, claims a neglected Fairford farm and makes Ellie and Ben part of its household. He recovers stolen cattle, develops a dairy and cheese business, and learns that peaceful human bonds can reduce the doom points that threaten to begin an apocalypse. His crop experiments and recovered mage research point toward food able to survive a mana-altered world. After fighting Haver, John later joins him and Red Coral on a tower expedition. Red Coral betrays them and sends attackers toward Sutton Farm, but John and Haver survive. The farm's defenders kill Reese, Sandra, and Grimes, and Ellie survives using dangerous curse magic. John recovers the expedition's ornate box and discovers water-magic scrolls. He also identifies Tower Master Kelvis as Storm Master Kelvis and learns of an inverted tower holding further research tied to the summoning. The farm is secure, but the apocalypse, Ellie's unfinished magical development, Earl Vosik, and the hidden tower remain unresolved.

recaps.text:
John Sutton arrives in the Robain Mountains after leaving the Royal Lepirian Army, intending to retire on a farm granted to him near Fairford. Village leader Holden confirms the deed but warns that the neglected property is not what its records promise. At the homestead, John finds Ellie and Ben living in poverty while Wale and his men claim the farm's six cows and bull under a supposed debt. John takes possession, allows the siblings to remain as workers, and gives Wale three days to return the livestock.

John also encounters the Red Coral Adventuring Team, whose stated search for an heirloom conceals an interest in a tower. Fairford treats Ellie as a witch, and she implicates Holden in the rumors that isolated her. George directs John to Wolf Den, where Wale serves a bandit leader known as the Wolf King. John goes there alone, defeats the defenders, and returns with every stolen animal. With the cattle restored and Ellie and Ben established in his household, his initial retirement quest is complete. Holden's conduct, Red Coral's surveillance, the Wolf King, and recurring warnings about an approaching apocalypse remain unresolved.

John learns that retirement is only one part of an apocalypse quest. If his doom reaches 100 points, the apocalypse begins, and his next task is to create wheat able to survive any environment. Because mana has ruined farmland elsewhere, he treats the assignment as a possible answer to famine. He starts ordinary plowing with the recovered bull and expands the dairy's storage.

At an abandoned quarry, John trades mana crystals with intelligent grimm for a metal chest and unusual flowers. The chest contains seeds, mage clothing, and a locked journal. John discovers that quiet companionship with Ellie can lower his doom, while destructive magic raises it. The bull grows more intelligent after consuming mana and tending a mana-seeking vine, and John names him Ferdie. Together they build a fortified underground cheese cellar that can shelter Ellie and Ben.

John opens the journal away from the farm. It describes Tower Master Kelvis's old plant-research tower and the hidden Shadow Tower created after repeated attacks. Experiments there produced plants that grow without sunlight and seek mana. The grimm flowers appear connected to that work, giving John and Ellie a possible path toward crops suited to the altered world.

Haver, the Wolf King, arrives with Wale, Jemal, Rosalee, and wolves. He claims the valley, demands the cattle, and orders John to surrender Ellie. John refuses. At the same time, caravan master Gofreid recognizes John's concealed identity and agrees to keep it secret while supplying the farm and marketing its cheese.

When Haver attacks, Rosalee's advance ambush fails. Haver then diverts most of his force toward the farm while Jemal and two wolves hold John back. John kills all three, but the violence increases his doom. Ellie helps him settle the dangerous mana surge afterward. Haver and Rosalee survive, later appearing openly in Fairford, where John bars them from his land and Haver accepts the boundary.

The household adds eleven hens and the formidable rooster Sigvald. Ben takes charge of the chickens, while John and Ellie plan more housing and dairy infrastructure. John's corn trials show the central agricultural problem: heavy mana makes crops grow rapidly, but the plants store it at dangerous levels instead of becoming safe food. The farm is becoming a lasting home for John, Ellie, and Ben, but the mana-resistant food quest and a pattern of murders in the valley remain open.

John buys 23 neglected cows from Keller and escorts the departing farmer safely out of the valley. He also purchases Keller's land, gaining a mountain spring, grazing rights, and a flooded iron mine. After recovering iron and forging pipes, John installs running hot and cold water, sanitation, and expanded dairy facilities. The mana-treated corn remains unsuitable for people, though it can feed animals.

Gofreid formalizes a distribution partnership for Sutton Farm cheese. The enlarged herd creates an urgent labor shortage, leading to the arrival of Thomas Little, John's former military adjutant. Thomas recognizes John's hidden identity, joins the household as a farmhand and defender, and agrees to train Ben. Evan is hired as another milker. With regular milk sales, cheese shipments, a planted wheat crop, and additional staff, Sutton Farm becomes a functioning commercial dairy.

Reese asks John to accompany Red Coral on an expedition to ruins in the mountains because the group has found signs of dangerous beasts. John agrees only to hear the team's proposal.

John departs with Red Coral, Haver, and Rosalee for a ruined academy tower. Rosalee secretly warns John to watch for betrayal. A larger-than-expected gnoll force drives the party through a sealed entrance, leaving the expedition trapped underground and dependent on a lower-level portal for escape. Thorn bears pursue them through the corridors. Tower traps decapitate Rosalee and the Red Coral monk, while John and Haver form a temporary pact after concluding that the local recruits are being used as expendable protection.

In the lowest hall, Catherine retrieves an ornate box that is supposedly needed before the portal can operate. The Red Coral members then attack John and Haver. Haver is poisoned, one of his wolves dies defending him, and the adventurers escape through the portal before destroying it from the far side. Catherine leaves with the box toward the capital, while Reese, Sandra, and Grimes head toward Sutton Farm. Grimes states his intention to take Ellie. John and Haver are stranded with eight thorn bears, and the farm faces an attack without either of them visibly present.

John reveals his full power and kills all eight thorn bears, increasing his apocalypse doom in the process. Haver survives Catherine's poison when his remaining bonded wolf sacrifices its spirit to heal him. The two men recover mage books, documents, a compass-like artifact, a quill, and a Mental Model skill scroll before escaping the tower. They pursue the betrayers, then split up. Haver races after the three adventurers bound for Sutton Farm, while John follows Catherine. He forces her to surrender the ornate box and access token and learns that Earl Vosik commissioned the retrieval. When she attacks again, John severs her leg, but her final fate is not established.

At Sutton Farm, Ellie delays Reese, Sandra, and Grimes until the defenders can act. Thomas Little kills Sandra, while Ferdie and Sigvald defeat Reese. Ellie draws Grimes into the woods, uses tea to bind him to a curse, and fatally stabs him. The mana strain nearly kills her, but John arrives and removes it. Haver sees that the farm has defended itself and leaves. All three attackers sent to the farm are dead, and Ellie's recovery is expected.

After the attack, Ellie explains that her grandmother attempted to transfer a witch class to her. Ellie's different affinity interrupted the ceremony, leaving her with limited mana capacity rather than a completed class. She also reveals that Ben is not her biological brother: she found him in ruins when they were children and later presented them as siblings. John invests all his unused skill points in Mental Model and determines that Ellie may still gain a mana-using class once she finds the right medium, with water the strongest current possibility.

John returns to the grimm exchange and opens recovered mage materials. He confirms that Tower Master Kelvis was also Storm Master Kelvis. Kelvis's testament says he participated in the world-changing summoning and concealed an inverted tower in the mountains, where his student's research remains. The compass and quill were left to help locate it. John also opens the ornate box and finds six water-magic spell scrolls. Sutton Farm ends the book secure and expanding, but John still faces the apocalypse threshold and the unfinished search for mana-compatible food. The inverted tower, Ellie's magical path, Catherine's uncertain fate, and Earl Vosik remain open.
